indicators.definition: 公司净利润除以流通在外的普通股股数，反映每股所获得的利润

indicators.name: EPS增长率
indicators.definition: 每股收益在一定时期内的年均增长率，用于衡量公司盈利的成长性

indicators.name: 稀释后每股收益 (Diluted EPS)
indicators.definition: 假设所有可转换证券、期权等全部转换为普通股后计算的每股收益，反映最保守的盈利水平

indicators.name: 平均每股收益 (Average EPS)
indicators.definition: 过去若干年（如5年或10年）每股收益的算术平均值，用于平滑周期性波动

indicators.name: 非经常性损益
indicators.definition: 一次性或非持续性的收入或支出项目，如资产出售收益、重组费用等，需从常规盈利分析中剔除

indicators.name: Current Ratio
indicators.definition: Current assets divided by current liabilities; measures short-term liquidity and financial strength

indicators.name: P/E (Price-to-Earnings Ratio)
indicators.definition: Current stock price divided by earnings per share; Graham recommends using average earnings over the past 3 years

indicators.name: P/B (Price-to-Book Ratio)
indicators.definition: Current stock price divided by book value per share; measures how much investors pay relative to net asset value

indicators.name: Debt-to-Equity Ratio
indicators.definition: Total long-term debt divided by shareholders' equity; measures financial leverage and conservative financing

indicators.name: EPS Growth (10-year)
indicators.definition: Percentage growth in earnings per share comparing the most recent 3-year average to the earliest 3-year average over a 10-year period

indicators.name: Dividend Record
indicators.definition: Number of consecutive years of uninterrupted dividend payments

indicators.name: Annual Revenue
indicators.definition: Total annual sales revenue used to gauge adequate enterprise size

indicators.name: Working Capital
indicators.definition: Current assets minus current liabilities; represents net liquid assets available

indicators.name: Net Current Asset Value (NCAV)
indicators.definition: Current assets minus total liabilities; Graham's measure of liquidation value or bargain threshold

indicators.name: PE (Price-to-Earnings Ratio)
indicators.definition: Market price per share divided by earnings per share; measures how much investors pay per dollar of earnings

indicators.name: Price-to-Book (P/B) or Price-to-Tangible-Book
indicators.definition: Market price per share divided by tangible book value per share; measures price relative to net asset value

indicators.name: Debt-to-Net-Current-Assets Ratio
indicators.definition: Total debt divided by net current assets; measures leverage relative to liquid asset base

indicators.name: Earnings Stability
indicators.definition: Consistency of positive earnings over the past five or more years

indicators.name: Earnings Growth
indicators.definition: Comparison of current earnings to earnings five years prior to ensure positive trend

indicators.name: Dividend Yield
indicators.definition: Annual dividends per share divided by price per share; indicates current income return

indicators.name: Conversion Premium
indicators.definition: 可转换债券的市场价格相对于其转换价值（将债券转换为股票后的市场价值）的溢价百分比

indicators.name: Conversion Ratio
indicators.definition: 每张可转换债券可以转换为普通股的股数

indicators.name: Conversion Value
indicators.definition: 可转换债券按当前股价转换为股票后的市场价值，等于转换比率乘以当前股价

indicators.name: Warrant Exercise Price
indicators.definition: 认股权证持有人购买标的股票时需支付的价格

indicators.name: Bond Yield
indicators.definition: 债券的到期收益率，用于衡量可转换债券相对于普通债券的收益率牺牲

indicators.name: Dilution Effect
indicators.definition: 可转换证券和认股权证转换/行权后对现有股东每股收益的稀释效应

indicators.name: Annual Sales (年销售额)
indicators.definition: 企业在一个财政年度内的总销售收入，用于衡量企业的经营规模

indicators.name: Total Assets (总资产)
indicators.definition: 企业资产负债表上的总资产金额，用于衡量公用事业企业的规模

indicators.name: Working Capital (Net Current Assets)
indicators.definition: 营运资本，等于流动资产减去流动负债（Current Assets - Current Liabilities），反映公司可用于日常运营的净流动资金

indicators.name: Long-term Debt
indicators.definition: 长期债务，指公司一年以上到期的债务总额

indicators.name: Debt to Equity Ratio (Book Value)
indicators.definition: 债务与股东权益比率，等于总债务除以账面价值的股东权益（Total Debt / Book Value of Equity），衡量公司财务杠杆水平

indicators.name: Book Value of Equity
indicators.definition: 股东权益的账面价值，即公司总资产减去总负债后的净资产

indicators.name: EPS (Earnings Per Share)
indicators.definition: Net income available to common shareholders divided by the number of outstanding shares, measured on an annual basis.

indicators.name: consecutive_dividend_years
indicators.definition: 公司连续支付现金股息的年数，中间无任何中断

indicators.name: EPS 三年平均值
indicators.definition: 连续三年每股收益的算术平均值，用于平滑单一年份的异常波动，更准确地反映公司真实盈利水平。

indicators.name: 十年每股收益增长率
indicators.definition: 以期初三年平均EPS为基准，期末三年平均EPS相对于基准的增长百分比，用于衡量公司长期盈利增长能力。

indicators.name: PE (基于三年平均盈利)
indicators.definition: 当前股价除以过去三年的平均每股收益（EPS），用于衡量股票相对于其历史盈利水平的估值是否合理。

indicators.name: P/B
indicators.definition: Price-to-Book Value ratio: the current stock price divided by the last reported book value per share

indicators.name: PE
indicators.definition: Price-to-Earnings ratio: the current stock price divided by earnings per share

indicators.name: PE * P/B
indicators.definition: The Graham combined ratio: the product of the PE ratio and the Price-to-Book ratio, which should not exceed 22.5 to ensure a margin of safety

indicators.name: Earnings Yield
indicators.definition: The inverse of the PE ratio (E/P), representing earnings per dollar of price paid, compared against high-grade bond yields

indicators.name: AA Bond Yield
indicators.definition: The yield on high-grade AA-rated bonds, used as a benchmark to determine acceptable PE ratios for stock investments

indicators.name: Financial Condition
indicators.definition: 公司的整体财务健康状况，通常通过资产负债表、流动性、偿债能力等综合评估

indicators.name: P/E (市盈率)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股收益的比率，Graham建议使用三年平均盈利来计算，以平滑周期性波动

indicators.name: E/P (盈利收益率)
indicators.definition: 每股收益与股价的比率，即市盈率的倒数，用于与债券收益率进行比较

indicators.name: Dividend Yield (股息收益率)
indicators.definition: 每股股息与股价的比率，反映投资者从股息中获得的回报

indicators.name: Net Asset Value per Share (每股净资产)
indicators.definition: 公司净资产除以总股数，反映每股的账面价值

indicators.name: NCA (净流动资产)
indicators.definition: 净流动资产，即流动资产减去全部负债，反映公司的清算价值

indicators.name: Current Ratio (流动比率)
indicators.definition: 流动资产与流动负债的比率（CA/CL），衡量公司短期偿债能力

indicators.name: Earnings Growth (盈利增长率)
indicators.definition: 比较不同时期的每股收益增长百分比，如近十年的盈利增长

indicators.name: Dividend Record (股息支付记录)
indicators.definition: 公司连续支付股息的年数，反映公司的稳定性和对股东回报的承诺

indicators.name: Price to Book Value (市净率)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股净资产价值的比率，衡量股票相对于账面价值的溢价程度

indicators.name: AA Corporate Bond Yield (AA级公司债收益率)
indicators.definition: AA评级公司债券的到期收益率，作为股票估值的基准参考

indicators.name: Aggregate Earnings Stability
indicators.definition: 过去十年内公司总体盈利的稳定性，衡量公司是否在较长时期内持续盈利而未出现亏损

indicators.name: Annual Deficit
indicators.definition: 公司年度净利润为负（亏损），即报告赤字，表明公司在该年度未能实现盈利

indicators.name: 三年平均值（Three-Year Average）
indicators.definition: 取连续三年的财务数据（如收益、营收等）的平均值，用以平滑年度波动，获得更稳定的增长衡量基准

indicators.name: 十年总增长率（Total Growth over a Decade）
indicators.definition: 比较相隔十年的两个三年平均值，计算总增长百分比，用于评估公司的长期增长能力

indicators.name: 年化增长率（Annualized Growth Rate）
indicators.definition: 将十年总增长率折算为每年的复合增长率，如77%的十年总增长约等于每年6%的增长

indicators.name: P/E (3-year average earnings)
indicators.definition: 年末股价与过去三年平均每股收益的比率，用于平滑单一年份盈利波动，衡量股票估值水平

indicators.name: P/B (Price to Book / Price to Net Asset Value)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股净资产价值的比率，格雷厄姆建议该比率不超过1.5倍

indicators.name: 过去增长率 (Past Growth Rate)
indicators.definition: 公司过去一段时间的盈利或收入增长率，用于评估公司历史成长性

indicators.name: P/E (Price/Earnings)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股收益的比率，可按当年或三年平均计算，用于衡量股票估值水平

indicators.name: Price/Book Value
indicators.definition: 股价与每股账面价值的比率，衡量市场价格相对于公司净资产的溢价或折价程度

indicators.name: Current Ratio (Current Assets / Current Liabilities)
indicators.definition: 流动资产与流动负债的比率，衡量公司短期偿债能力

indicators.name: Net Current Assets / Debt
indicators.definition: 净流动资产与债务的比率，衡量公司流动资产覆盖债务的能力

indicators.name: Stability Index
indicators.definition: 衡量公司盈利稳定性的指标（格雷厄姆定义，详见原书第338页）

indicators.name: Growth (Earnings Growth)
indicators.definition: 每股收益的增长率，如1960年至1970年的增长百分比，衡量公司盈利增长能力

indicators.name: Market Capitalization
indicators.definition: 公司总市值，用于筛选公司规模，防御型投资者应排除市值低于20亿美元的公司

indicators.name: Earnings (盈利)
indicators.definition: 公司的盈利记录，用于评估卖出价格与盈利之间的关系

indicators.name: Assets (资产)
indicators.definition: 公司资产价值，用于量化方法中评估内在价值

indicators.name: Dividends (股息)
indicators.definition: 公司支付的股息，作为量化选股方法中的可衡量关系之一

indicators.name: Margin of Safety (安全边际)
indicators.definition: 当前指示内在价值高于市场价格的幅度，用于吸收未来可能的不利发展

indicators.name: Price (市场价格)
indicators.definition: 股票当前的市场交易价格，保护型投资者在研究时特别关注的核心要素

indicators.name: 五年财务健康状况比较
indicators.definition: 对公司至少五年的财务报表数据进行纵向比较，评估其财务健康趋势和稳定性

indicators.name: 委托声明书（Proxy Statement）
indicators.definition: 公司向股东披露的文件，包含管理层薪酬、公司治理结构、股东投票事项等关键信息，是评估公司治理质量的重要依据

indicators.name: Debt to Net Current Assets Ratio
indicators.definition: Total debt divided by net current assets (current assets minus current liabilities); should not exceed 110% for industrial companies.

indicators.name: Earnings Growth Rate
indicators.definition: Rate at which a company's earnings grow over time. Extremely few companies sustain high uninterrupted growth for long periods.

indicators.name: S&P 500 Average Return
indicators.definition: Benchmark return of the broad market index used to evaluate whether individual stock selection or fund management adds value. Approximately 15% annual return in 1951-1960 and 6% in 1961-1970.

indicators.name: Net Current Assets
indicators.definition: Current assets minus current liabilities; represents the working capital available to the company.

indicators.name: Stock Price
indicators.definition: The market price per share of the stock. Very low-priced stocks (below ~$1) carry delisting risk and high transaction costs.

indicators.name: Net Income
indicators.definition: 公司净利润，即总收入减去所有费用、税收后的最终盈利，用于判断公司是否出现亏损（赤字）

indicators.name: Price/Net Tangible Asset Ratio
indicators.definition: 股票价格与每股有形资产净值的比率，格雷厄姆要求低于120%

indicators.name: PE (Price/Earnings Ratio)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股收益的比率，低市盈率是格雷厄姆选股的核心标准之一

indicators.name: Book Value Per Share
indicators.definition: 每股账面价值，用于衡量股票价格相对于公司资产的折价或溢价程度

indicators.name: S&P Quality Ranking
indicators.definition: 标准普尔基于过去八年盈利和股息的稳定性与增长性给出的质量评级，从A+（最高）到较低等级

indicators.name: Goodwill Component
indicators.definition: 商誉成分，即市场价格超过账面价值的部分，反映市场对公司无形资产和品牌价值的估值

indicators.name: Earnings Per Share (EPS)
indicators.definition: 每股收益，用于计算市盈率和评估公司盈利能力

indicators.name: Net Current Asset Value Per Share
indicators.definition: 每股净流动资产价值，即流动资产减去全部负债后除以总股数，用于判断股票是否被严重低估

indicators.name: Price to Book Value Ratio
indicators.definition: 股价与每股账面价值的比率，用于衡量市场对公司资产的估值程度

indicators.name: Merger Spread
indicators.definition: 并购套利价差，即收购报价与目标公司当前市场价格之间的差额，反映潜在收益和交易风险

indicators.name: Annualized Return Rate
indicators.definition: 年化收益率，用于评估特殊情况投资（workouts）的回报，考虑时间因素

indicators.name: Liquid Assets Ratio
indicators.definition: 流动资产占总资产的比例，在清算情况下尤为重要，流动资产比例越高，实现账面价值的可能性越大

indicators.name: 转换溢价/折价
indicators.definition: 可转换证券的市场价格与其转换为普通股后价值之间的差异，用于衡量转换特权的价值

indicators.name: 优先股/债券信用评级
indicators.definition: 如标准普尔评级（A级或以上 vs 低于A级），用于衡量高级证券的信用质量和违约风险

indicators.name: 收益率（Yield）
indicators.definition: 可转换证券的利息或股息收益率，通常低于同等质量的非可转换证券，差额即为转换特权的隐含成本

indicators.name: 价格相对发行价的变动幅度
indicators.definition: 衡量证券自发行以来的价格表现，用于评估投资回报，如表16-1和16-2所示的价格变动分类

indicators.name: True Market Price (修正后的真实市场价格)
indicators.definition: 将公司所有流通认股权证的市场价值按每股分摊后，加上普通股当前市场价格，得到的修正股价。反映了投资者获取公司全部股权的真实成本。

indicators.name: Warrant Value Per Share (每股认股权证价值)
indicators.definition: 公司所有流通认股权证的总市场价值除以普通股总股数，代表每股普通股中隐含的认股权证稀释成本。

indicators.name: Warrant Market Value (认股权证市场价值)
indicators.definition: 公司所有流通在外的认股权证按市场价格计算的总价值，反映了潜在的股权稀释规模。

indicators.name: P/E Ratio (Diluted for Warrants)
indicators.definition: Price-to-earnings ratio calculated by adjusting the stock price upward to account for warrant dilution, rather than simply diluting earnings per share, to reflect the true cost of potential dilution to existing shareholders.

indicators.name: Earnings Per Share (EPS) - Before and After Special Items
indicators.definition: Net income per share calculated both before and after special/extraordinary charges, used to assess the impact of one-time items on valuation.

indicators.name: 年度运营费用率
indicators.definition: 基金每年收取的管理费和运营费用占基金资产的百分比

indicators.name: 现金收益率（Cash Yield）
indicators.definition: 可转换债券的票面利息收益率，用于评估债券的收入回报

indicators.name: 转换概率
indicators.definition: 可转换债券被转换为普通股的可能性，低转换概率意味着债券的股权特征价值较低

indicators.name: 净资产价值折价/溢价
indicators.definition: 封闭式基金市场价格相对于其净资产价值的折价或溢价幅度

indicators.name: 看涨期权权利金（Call Premium）
indicators.definition: 卖出看涨期权时收到的现金收入，作为对放弃上涨潜力的补偿

indicators.name: Bond Default Risk
indicators.definition: Assessment of a company's ability to meet its bond obligations, based on financial strength indicators

indicators.name: Debt-to-Size Ratio in Acquisitions
indicators.definition: The ratio of debt incurred in an acquisition relative to the acquiring company's size, indicating financial overextension

indicators.name: Revenue and Asset Size vs. Financial Health
indicators.definition: Comparison of a company's gross revenues and total assets against its actual financial stability and solvency

indicators.name: Stock Price vs. Fundamental Value
indicators.definition: Comparison of market price to underlying financial condition to identify overvaluation in financially weak companies

indicators.name: Interest Coverage Ratio (Times Interest Earned)
indicators.definition: 利息保障倍数，即息税前利润（EBIT）除以利息费用，衡量公司偿付债务利息的能力。倍数越高，说明公司偿债能力越强，债券越安全。

indicators.name: Interest Coverage Ratio (Before Tax)
indicators.definition: 税前利润与利息费用的比率，衡量公司支付债务利息的能力。保守要求铁路债券至少达到5倍。

indicators.name: Interest Coverage Ratio (After Tax)
indicators.definition: 税后利润与利息费用的比率，铁路债券的最低要求为2.9倍。该指标基于正常所得税率下的税前5倍覆盖率推导而来。

indicators.name: Income Tax Payment History
indicators.definition: 公司实际缴纳所得税的历史记录，长期未缴税可能掩盖真实盈利能力不足的问题，需特别警惕。

indicators.name: Income Tax Paid / Income Tax Expense
indicators.definition: 公司在一定期间内实际缴纳的所得税金额，反映公司盈利的真实性和纳税义务的履行情况

indicators.name: Effective Tax Rate
indicators.definition: 实际所得税费用与税前利润的比率，异常低的有效税率（尤其为零）可能暗示报告盈利存在虚假或操纵

indicators.name: Earnings Quality
indicators.definition: 衡量公司报告盈利的可靠性和可持续性，可通过所得税缴纳与报告利润的匹配程度来辅助判断

indicators.name: Interest Coverage Ratio (税前利息保障倍数)
indicators.definition: 税前利润除以利息费用，衡量发行人偿付债券利息的能力。例如Penn Central仅1.98倍，而Pennsylvania Electric Co.达4.20倍

indicators.name: Bond Price Range
indicators.definition: 债券在一定时期内的价格波动区间，用于评估债券的市场价值和流动性

indicators.name: Coupon Rate (票面利率)
indicators.definition: 债券的固定利息率，用于比较不同债券的收益水平

indicators.name: Dividend Payout Ratio
indicators.definition: 股利支付率，即公司支付的股利占净利润的比例，反映公司将多少利润回馈给股东

indicators.name: Retained Earnings Growth Efficiency
indicators.definition: 留存收益增长效率，衡量公司留存的每一美元收益是否带来了相应的每股收益或市值增长

indicators.name: EPS Growth Rate
indicators.definition: 每股收益增长率，用于评估管理层留存利润再投资的效果

indicators.name: ROE
indicators.definition: 净资产收益率，衡量公司利用股东权益创造利润的能力，与股利政策决策密切相关

indicators.name: 非经常性损失（Extraordinary Charges）
indicators.definition: 公司报告为'非经常性'或'特殊'的成本和损失，可能被用来美化经常性盈利数据。

indicators.name: EPS（每股收益）
indicators.definition: 每股收益，需要关注其是否剔除了重大成本和损失后的美化数字。

indicators.name: Transportation Ratio
indicators.definition: 铁路公司的运输比率，即运输费用占运输收入的百分比。比率越高，说明运营效率越低、盈利能力越差。Penn Central的47.5%远高于Norfolk & Western的35.2%，表明其运营状况极差。

indicators.name: Operating Ratio (Transportation Ratio)
indicators.definition: Measures the expenses of running operations divided by the company's revenue; for railroads, even a ratio of 70% would be considered excellent

indicators.name: Interest Coverage Ratio (Times Earned)
indicators.definition: Net income before taxes and interest divided by interest charges; measures ability to service debt obligations

indicators.name: Equity to Long-term Debt Ratio
indicators.definition: Shareholders' equity divided by long-term debt; measures financial leverage and solvency

indicators.name: Earned Per Share
indicators.definition: Net income available to common shareholders divided by shares outstanding

indicators.name: Debt Payable Within 1 Year
indicators.definition: Short-term debt obligations due within the current fiscal year

indicators.name: Total Assets
indicators.definition: Sum of all assets on the balance sheet

indicators.name: Shareholders' Equity
indicators.definition: Net assets belonging to shareholders, calculated excluding debt-discount as an asset and deducting preferred stock at redemption value

indicators.name: 一次性费用与准备金（One-time Charges and Reserves）
indicators.definition: 公司在某一特定年份集中计提的特殊费用和准备金，常被用于'大洗澡'式会计操纵，将未来可能的费用提前集中在亏损年份确认。

indicators.name: 经营利润/亏损（Operating Profit/Deficit）
indicators.definition: 公司核心经营活动产生的利润或亏损，不包括一次性项目，用于衡量公司主营业务的真实盈利能力。

indicators.name: 盈利质量（Earnings Quality）
indicators.definition: 衡量公司报告盈利的真实性和可持续性，需关注一次性费用集中计提后是否人为制造了后续年份的盈利增长假象。

indicators.name: 净有形资产每股价值 (Net Tangible Assets Per Share)
indicators.definition: 公司总资产减去无形资产（如商誉）、减去所有负债和优先股后，除以普通股股数得到的每股价值

indicators.name: 市价与净有形资产比率 (Price to Net Tangible Assets)
indicators.definition: 股票市场价格与每股净有形资产的比率，用于衡量市场对公司有形资产的溢价程度

indicators.name: 商誉 (Goodwill)
indicators.definition: 资产负债表中的无形资产项目，通常因收购溢价产生，在评估公司真实有形价值时应予以扣除

indicators.name: 债券折价资产 (Bond Discount Asset)
indicators.definition: 债券以低于面值发行时产生的折价，被列为资产负债表上的资产项目，但并非真实的有形资产

indicators.name: 重组费用（Restructuring Charges）
indicators.definition: 公司在重组过程中一次性计提的大额费用，常被用于'大洗澡'会计手法，将所有负面因素集中在一个会计期间，以美化后续期间的盈利表现

indicators.name: 债券市场价格与面值的折价/溢价
indicators.definition: 债券当前市场价格与其面值之间的差异，折价债券的市场价格低于面值，不能简单假设其会回归面值

indicators.name: Bank Loans (Short-term Debt)
indicators.definition: 公司从银行获得的短期贷款总额，反映短期融资依赖程度

indicators.name: Combined/Total Debt
indicators.definition: 短期银行贷款与长期债务的合计总额，衡量公司整体负债水平

indicators.name: Debt Growth Rate (YOY)
indicators.definition: 债务的同比增长率，用于衡量公司举债扩张的速度是否过快

indicators.name: Interest Coverage Ratio
indicators.definition: Ratio of earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) to interest charges, measuring a company's ability to service its debt

indicators.name: Tangible Book Value (Tangible Stock Capital)
indicators.definition: Total stockholders' equity minus intangible assets, representing the tangible net worth of the company

indicators.name: Bond Market Price vs Face Value
indicators.definition: The market trading price of a bond relative to its face (par) value, indicating market confidence in the issuer's creditworthiness

indicators.name: Net Earnings per Share
indicators.definition: Net income divided by shares outstanding, measuring profitability on a per-share basis

indicators.name: Deferred Debt Expense
indicators.definition: A balance sheet item representing capitalized costs related to debt issuance or restructuring that are amortized over time; in this context listed as an asset of $58,600,000 which may overstate true asset value.

indicators.name: Stockholders' Equity
indicators.definition: The net value of a company belonging to shareholders, here noted at $40,200,000; should be evaluated in relation to potentially inflated assets such as deferred debt expenses.

indicators.name: 有形股东权益（Tangible Shareholders' Equity）
indicators.definition: 剔除不合理资产项目（如递延债务费用）并纳入遗漏权益项目后的真实股东权益

indicators.name: 每股有形权益（Tangible Equity Per Share）
indicators.definition: 调整后的有形股东权益除以流通股数，反映每股的真实净资产价值

indicators.name: 递延债务费用摊销（Amortization of Deferred Debt Expense）
indicators.definition: 因低价发行债券产生的折价部分在债券存续期内的摊销费用，需关注其是否被用于会计操纵

indicators.name: 子公司投资权益超过成本的摊销
indicators.definition: 收购子公司时权益超过收购成本部分的摊销收入，属于非经常性的会计收入项目

indicators.name: 认股权证市值与流通股市值比率
indicators.definition: 并购中发行的认股权证总市值与收购方流通股总市值的比率，用于评估交易结构的合理性

indicators.name: Margin of Safety
indicators.definition: 内在价值与市场价格之间的差额，是投资的核心概念，用于缓冲估值误差和市场不利变动带来的风险

indicators.name: Earnings Yield (E/P)
indicators.definition: 每股收益与股价的比率，即市盈率的倒数，用于与债券收益率比较以衡量股票的安全边际

indicators.name: Intrinsic Value
indicators.definition: 基于公司资产、盈利、股息和未来前景等因素估算的证券真实价值

indicators.name: Earning Power
indicators.definition: 公司的盈利能力，通常以多年平均盈利来衡量，是评估安全边际的关键因素

indicators.name: 债券市场价格与面值比率
indicators.definition: 公司债券的市场交易价格相对于其面值的比率，低于40%通常预示严重的财务困难

indicators.name: 现金余额
indicators.definition: 公司年末持有的现金及等价物总额，用于评估短期偿债能力

indicators.name: 短期到期债务
indicators.definition: 公司在未来一年内需要偿还的债务总额

indicators.name: 现金与短期债务比率
indicators.definition: 公司现金余额与短期到期债务的比率，反映短期偿债能力。NVF案例中仅为700,000/4,400,000≈16%，极度危险

indicators.name: 债券价格（占面值百分比）
indicators.definition: 公司债券的市场交易价格相对于其面值的百分比，低于面值交易表明市场对公司偿债能力存在担忧

indicators.name: 股息支付情况
indicators.definition: 公司是否向普通股股东支付股息，不支付股息可能表明公司现金流紧张或优先用于其他用途

indicators.name: 债务退出/置换规模
indicators.definition: 公司通过发行普通股来退出（retire）债券和认股权证的金额规模，反映公司资本结构调整的力度

indicators.name: 认股权证（Warrants）
indicators.definition: 赋予持有人在特定价格购买公司普通股权利的证券，公司回收认股权证可减少未来潜在的股权稀释

indicators.name: Warrants
indicators.definition: 权证，即以特定价格在未来购买公司股票的权利，属于衍生金融工具

indicators.name: 每股收益（EPS）
indicators.definition: 公司净利润除以流通在外的普通股数量，需注意是否经过稀释调整以及是否受会计方法变更影响

indicators.name: 折旧率
indicators.definition: 公司对固定资产计提折旧的比率，降低折旧率会减少费用从而虚增利润

indicators.name: 养老金成本
indicators.definition: 公司为员工养老金计划计提的费用，变更计算方法可能显著影响报告利润

indicators.name: P/E (Price/Earnings Ratio)
indicators.definition: Stock price divided by earnings per share; must be calculated on both basic and fully diluted basis to account for warrants and options

indicators.name: Diluted EPS
indicators.definition: Earnings per share calculated assuming all convertible securities, warrants, and options are exercised, increasing shares outstanding and reducing EPS

indicators.name: Price-to-Book Ratio
indicators.definition: Market capitalization divided by book value of equity; extreme ratios (e.g., 20x) on companies with minimal earnings indicate speculative pricing

indicators.name: Equity-to-Debt Ratio
indicators.definition: Aggregate market value of equity (stock + warrants) relative to total bonded debt; a very low ratio indicates dangerous leverage

indicators.name: Insider Selling Ratio at IPO
indicators.definition: Proportion of IPO shares sold by insiders vs. shares sold for company account; high insider selling relative to company issuance is a warning sign

indicators.name: PB (Price-to-Book Ratio)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股账面价值的比率，用于衡量市场对公司净资产的估值溢价程度

indicators.name: Capital Consumption Rate
indicators.definition: Rate at which a company's losses consume its total equity including recently raised capital; indicates financial distress severity

indicators.name: IPO Price Premium to Book Value
indicators.definition: The ratio of IPO offering price to pre-offering book value per share; high premiums mean investors are overpaying relative to tangible assets

indicators.name: 资本赤字（Capital Deficit）
indicators.definition: 公司负债超过资产，股东权益为负值，表明公司处于资不抵债状态

indicators.name: 净亏损（Net Loss）
indicators.definition: 公司在一定期间内的经营亏损额，持续亏损是公司财务恶化的重要信号

indicators.name: 发行价与市场同类公司估值对比
indicators.definition: 新股发行价格与同类已上市公司市场价格的比较，用于判断IPO定价是否合理

indicators.name: 股东权益（Total Equity）
indicators.definition: 公司总资产减去总负债后的剩余价值，反映公司的净资产状况

indicators.name: Goodwill
indicators.definition: The excess of acquisition cost over the book value of acquired assets; extremely high goodwill relative to acquisition price signals overpayment, especially when the target has no revenues or customers

indicators.name: Customer Financing / Vendor Financing
indicators.definition: Loans or guarantees extended by a company to its own customers to finance purchases of its products; rapid growth in customer financing suggests artificially inflated revenues

indicators.name: Capitalized Software Development Costs
indicators.definition: Software development expenses treated as a capital asset on the balance sheet rather than expensed against earnings; can be used to inflate reported profits

indicators.name: Nonrecurring Charges (MORON)
indicators.definition: Merger, restructuring, or other charges labeled as nonrecurring; when they appear repeatedly, they represent ongoing costs of doing business and should be treated as recurring expenses

indicators.name: Impairment of Long-Lived Assets (CHILLA)
indicators.definition: Write-downs of long-lived assets when their carrying value exceeds fair value; chronic impairment charges suggest serial overpayment for acquisitions

indicators.name: Write-off of Purchased In-Process R&D (WOOPIPRAD)
indicators.definition: Immediate expensing of acquired in-process research and development; used to minimize goodwill allocated to the balance sheet and reduce future charges against earnings

indicators.name: Accounts Receivable Growth vs Revenue Growth
indicators.definition: Comparing the growth rate of receivables to revenue growth; receivables growing faster than revenues may indicate aggressive revenue recognition or collection problems

indicators.name: Acquirer-to-Target Size Ratio
indicators.definition: Ratio of the acquiring company's assets and revenues to the target's; a very small acquirer taking over a much larger target is a red flag for value destruction

indicators.name: Debt-to-Assets Ratio
indicators.definition: Total debt divided by total assets; measures the proportion of a company's assets financed by debt and indicates financial leverage risk

indicators.name: Dividend Continuity
indicators.definition: The number of consecutive years a company has paid dividends; a long track record indicates financial stability and sound management

indicators.name: Asset Growth Rate
indicators.definition: The rate at which a company's total assets have grown over a given period; excessively rapid growth may indicate reckless expansion

indicators.name: Debt Growth Rate
indicators.definition: The rate at which a company's total debt has grown over a given period; rapid debt growth proportional to asset growth signals high leverage risk

indicators.name: Business Diversification / Conglomerate Risk
indicators.definition: Qualitative assessment of whether a company has expanded into many unrelated business lines, which can signal management overreach and operational risk

indicators.name: 内在价值 (Intrinsic Value)
indicators.definition: 基于企业未来现金流、资产价值、盈利能力等基本面因素计算出的企业真实价值

indicators.name: 安全边际 (Margin of Safety)
indicators.definition: 买入价格与内在价值之间的差额，作为投资的安全缓冲

indicators.name: 市场价格与价值的折扣率
indicators.definition: 当前市场价格相对于内在价值的折扣程度，折扣越大安全边际越高

indicators.name: 优先股票面价值 (Par Value)
indicators.definition: 优先股在公司章程中规定的每股名义价值，可能与实际经济价值或清算价值存在重大差异。

indicators.name: 优先股年度股息 (Preferred Dividend)
indicators.definition: 优先股每年有权获得的固定股息金额，反映了优先股的实际经济负担。

indicators.name: 负债分类与账面价值 (Liability Classification & Carrying Value)
indicators.definition: 优先股在资产负债表上的分类方式及其入账金额，当以极低的票面价值列为负债时，可能严重低估公司的实际义务。

indicators.name: Common Stock Value
indicators.definition: The stated value of common stock on the balance sheet, representing the par or stated value per share multiplied by the number of shares issued.

indicators.name: Treasury Stock (Reacquired Stock)
indicators.definition: The cost of shares that a company has repurchased from shareholders, recorded as a deduction from stockholders' equity on the balance sheet.

indicators.name: Stock-Option Warrants
indicators.definition: 认股权证（stock-option warrants）是赋予持有人在特定条件下以约定价格购买公司股票的权利。此处涉及三个系列的认股权证，合计可购买1,578,000股。

indicators.name: Debt to Equity Ratio
indicators.definition: Total liabilities divided by book value of common equity; measures financial leverage and risk

indicators.name: Total Capitalization
indicators.definition: Sum of market value of equity (including warrants) plus debt plus preferred stock; represents the total enterprise value

indicators.name: Net to Gross Revenue Ratio
indicators.definition: Net income divided by gross revenues; measures overall profitability and operational efficiency

indicators.name: Warrant Dilution
indicators.definition: The potential dilution from outstanding warrants that can be exercised to purchase additional shares, which reduces per-share value

indicators.name: Net/Sales (Profit Margin)
indicators.definition: Net income as a percentage of sales revenue, measuring profitability per dollar of revenue.

indicators.name: Earnings/Book Value (Return on Equity)
indicators.definition: Net income divided by book value, measuring how efficiently a company generates profits from shareholders' equity.

indicators.name: Current Assets/Liabilities (Current Ratio)
indicators.definition: Ratio of current assets to current liabilities, measuring short-term liquidity and financial health.

indicators.name: Working Capital/Debt
indicators.definition: Ratio of working capital to total debt, measuring the company's ability to cover its debt obligations with liquid assets.

indicators.name: Total Capitalization at Market
indicators.definition: Market value of common stock plus debt, representing the total market-based valuation of the company's capital structure.

indicators.name: Price/Earnings Ratio Differential
indicators.definition: 不同公司之间市盈率的差异，用于比较估值是否合理反映了成长性和盈利能力的差异

indicators.name: Revenue Growth via Acquisitions
indicators.definition: 通过收购实现的收入增长占比，用于评估公司有机增长能力与收购驱动增长的区别

indicators.name: Price Range / Price Fluctuation
indicators.definition: 股票在特定时期内的价格波动范围，用于衡量波动性和投资者需承受的风险

indicators.name: Market Value (Market Cap)
indicators.definition: 公司总市值，股价乘以总股本，用于衡量公司规模和市场对其的整体估值

indicators.name: Revenue
indicators.definition: 营业收入，公司在一定时期内的总销售收入

indicators.name: PB
indicators.definition: 市净率，股价除以每股账面价值。Ball的PB为1.7倍，Stryker高达16倍

indicators.name: Revenue Growth Rate
indicators.definition: 营收增长率。Stryker季度销售增长15%

indicators.name: Total Market Value
indicators.definition: 公司总市值，股价乘以总股本数

indicators.name: Book Value
indicators.definition: 账面价值，公司有形资产净值。Ball账面价值11亿美元，Stryker为5.7亿美元

indicators.name: Debt Level
indicators.definition: 债务水平。Ball有超过9亿美元债务，需在估值时考虑

indicators.name: Dividend Rate
indicators.definition: 公司每股支付的股息金额

indicators.name: Market Price
indicators.definition: 股票的市场交易价格，长期不令人满意的市场价格可能反映管理层的无能

indicators.name: Return on Retained Earnings
indicators.definition: 留存利润再投资所产生的回报率，用于判断公司保留利润而非分红是否对股东有利

indicators.name: Profitability vs Industry Peers
indicators.definition: A company's profitability metrics (margins, ROE, ROA) compared against similar firms in the same industry to assess management efficiency

indicators.name: Insider Transactions / Related-Party Transactions
indicators.definition: Transactions between the company and its executives, directors, or their affiliated entities, disclosed in the proxy statement, which may indicate conflicts of interest

indicators.name: Management Compensation and Stock Ownership
indicators.definition: Details of how managers and directors are compensated and how much stock they own, disclosed in the proxy statement

indicators.name: Capital Allocation Efficiency
indicators.definition: Whether management deploys shareholders' capital in forms most suitable to shareholders' interests, including returning excess capital rather than hoarding it

indicators.name: Board Independence
indicators.definition: The degree to which a company's board of directors includes truly independent, professional directors with wide business experience who can objectively evaluate management

indicators.name: Share Repurchase Activity
indicators.definition: The dollar amount and timing of a company's stock buyback programs, used to assess whether management is buying back shares at favorable (undervalued) or unfavorable (overvalued) prices.

indicators.name: Stock Option Dilution Percentage
indicators.definition: The percentage of total shares outstanding that are granted to executives and employees via stock options, indicating potential dilution to existing shareholders.

indicators.name: Tax Benefits from Stock Options
indicators.definition: The tax deductions a company receives when employees exercise stock options, treated as compensation expense by the IRS but not reflected as an expense on the income statement.

indicators.name: Dividend Payout vs. Buyback Ratio
indicators.definition: The ratio of cash dividends paid to share repurchases, indicating whether the company favors returning cash directly to shareholders or buying back stock (which may benefit option-holding executives).

indicators.name: Earnings Volatility
indicators.definition: The variability of a company's profitability over time. Companies with buyback programs but no dividends tend to have at least twice the earnings volatility of dividend-paying companies, which increases stock option values for management.

indicators.name: Executive Compensation Structure
indicators.definition: The proportion of executive pay that comes from stock options and noncash awards versus cash compensation. Currently about two-thirds of executive compensation is noncash, compared to one-third thirty years ago.

indicators.name: 税后收益率
indicators.definition: 扣除适用税率后的实际投资回报率，反映投资者真正获得的净收益

indicators.name: 资本利得
indicators.definition: 证券卖出价格与买入价格之间的差额，分为短期资本利得和长期资本利得，适用不同税率

indicators.name: 股息收入
indicators.definition: 持有股票所获得的分红收入，需按相应税率缴纳所得税

indicators.name: 固定费用覆盖倍数（Fixed Charge Coverage Ratio）
indicators.definition: 税前利润与总固定费用（利息等）的比率，用于衡量债券的安全边际

indicators.name: 企业价值与债务比率（Enterprise Value to Debt）
indicators.definition: 企业总价值与债务金额的比率，反映债券持有人在企业价值缩水时的缓冲空间

indicators.name: 收益率（Earnings Yield）
indicators.definition: 每股收益除以股价（即市盈率的倒数），格雷厄姆称之为'盈利能力'（earning power）。市盈率11倍对应约9%的收益率

indicators.name: PE（市盈率）
indicators.definition: 股价与每股收益的比率，低市盈率意味着更高的收益率和更大的安全边际

indicators.name: 安全边际（Margin of Safety）
indicators.definition: 股票收益率与债券利率之间的差额，或企业盈利能力超出固定费用的部分，是防止投资损失的核心缓冲

indicators.name: 债券利率（Bond Rate）
indicators.definition: 当前优质债券的到期收益率，作为股票收益率的比较基准

indicators.name: Intrinsic/Business Value
indicators.definition: The true underlying value of a business based on its assets, earnings, dividends, and future prospects, as opposed to its current market price

indicators.name: Price vs Value Discrepancy
indicators.definition: The gap between what a business is worth (its intrinsic value) and the price at which small pieces of that business (shares) trade in the stock market

indicators.name: 内在价值（Intrinsic Value）
indicators.definition: 基于企业基本面分析得出的企业真实价值，与市场价格进行比较以寻找折价机会

indicators.name: 价格与价值折扣（Discount from Value）
indicators.definition: 股票市场价格相对于其内在价值的折扣幅度，折扣越大安全边际越高

indicators.name: S&P 500 复合收益率
indicators.definition: 标准普尔500指数的复合年化收益率，用作价值投资者业绩的基准比较

indicators.name: 超额年化收益（Alpha）
indicators.definition: 投资组合相对于基准指数的年化超额回报，如Sequoia基金每年超越S&P约4个百分点

indicators.name: Annual Rate of Return
indicators.definition: 投资组合在特定时间段内的年化收益率，用于衡量养老基金（包含债券和股票组合）的投资绩效

indicators.name: Becker Large Plan Median
indicators.definition: Becker大型养老金计划的中位数收益率，作为同类机构投资者的业绩基准

indicators.name: S&P 500 Return
indicators.definition: 标准普尔500指数的年度收益率，作为美国大盘股市场的基准回报率

indicators.name: Rolling Multi-Year Return
indicators.definition: 滚动多年期年化收益率，用于评估不同持有期限下投资组合的长期表现稳定性

indicators.name: 短期资本利得/损失
indicators.definition: 持有期不超过规定期限（通常一年以内）的资产出售所产生的利得或损失

indicators.name: 长期资本利得/损失
indicators.definition: 持有期超过规定期限（通常一年以上）的资产出售所产生的利得或损失

indicators.name: 普通收入
indicators.definition: 包括利息、股息等按普通所得税率征税的投资收入

indicators.name: 免税债券收入
indicators.definition: 州、市政及类似债券产生的利息收入，免联邦所得税

indicators.name: Short-term Capital Gain/Loss
indicators.definition: Net gain or loss from the sale of assets held for one year or less, taxed as ordinary income.

indicators.name: Long-term Capital Gain/Loss
indicators.definition: Net gain or loss from the sale of assets held for more than one year, taxed at a favorable rate (generally 20%).

indicators.name: Capital-Gain Dividends
indicators.definition: Distributions from mutual funds representing profits from the sale of long-term holdings, treated as long-term capital gains by shareholders.

indicators.name: Net Capital Loss Deduction
indicators.definition: The amount of net capital losses that can be deducted from ordinary income, capped at $3,000 per year with excess carried forward.

indicators.name: Book Value (账面价值)
indicators.definition: 公司有形资产减去负债后的净值，用于判断股票价格是否有实际资产支撑。需注意区分有形资产与无形资产（商誉/水分）。

indicators.name: Dividend (股息)
indicators.definition: 公司向股东支付的现金分红。稳定可靠的股息是传统投资型股票的重要特征。

indicators.name: Earnings Stability (盈利稳定性)
indicators.definition: 公司盈利在不同年份间的波动程度。盈利大幅波动是投机性的标志，盈利稳定则是投资性的标志。

indicators.name: Credit Rating (信用评级)
indicators.definition: 对公司偿债能力和财务健康状况的评级，信用评级最好的公司通常具有最佳投资特征。

indicators.name: Goodwill / Intangible Assets (商誉/无形资产)
indicators.definition: 资产负债表中超出有形资产的部分，可能代表品牌价值和未来盈利能力，也可能是虚增的'水分'。需要结合实际盈利能力判断其真实价值。

indicators.name: Growth Component (增长成分)
indicators.definition: 股票市场价格中超出当前盈利和资产所能解释的部分，反映市场对公司未来增长的预期定价。该成分具有高度不确定性和波动性。

indicators.name: Net Assets Per Share
indicators.definition: 每股净资产，公司净资产除以流通股数，反映每股的账面价值

indicators.name: Sales/Revenue
indicators.definition: 公司营业收入，反映公司业务规模和增长趋势

indicators.name: 五年盈利记录
indicators.definition: 公司过去五年的盈利历史，用于评估盈利的稳定性和持续性

indicators.name: 资产价值（净资产）
indicators.definition: 公司的账面资产价值，反映公司实际拥有的净资产规模

indicators.name: 当年盈亏状况
indicators.definition: 公司当前财年是否盈利或亏损（deficit），用于判断基本面是否支撑股价

indicators.name: Bond Market Value vs Par Value
indicators.definition: 债券市场价值与面值的比率。NVF发行的5%债券市场价值仅为面值的43%，反映了市场对债券实际价值的评估远低于票面金额。

indicators.name: Warrant Value
indicators.definition: 认股权证的市场价值。NVF权证允许以每股22美元购买NVF股票，权证市场报价为每涉及NVF股份10美元。

indicators.name: Acquisition Premium/Discount
indicators.definition: 收购价格与标的公司账面价值之间的差额。本例中每股差额为15美元（60美元账面价值减45美元收购对价），总差额约2100万美元。

indicators.name: Bond Discount Amortization
indicators.definition: The annual deduction from income resulting from amortizing the difference between a bond's face value and its issuance (or deemed sale) price, effectively representing the implicit interest cost on the proceeds of a discounted bond issue.

indicators.name: Excess of Equity over Cost Amortization
indicators.definition: An annual credit to income representing one-tenth of the difference between the cost price paid for an acquired stock and its higher book value, the reverse of goodwill amortization where acquisition price exceeds book value.

indicators.name: Effective Interest Rate on Bond Proceeds
indicators.definition: The actual annual interest rate borne by the issuer when bonds are issued at a deep discount; in this case approximately 15% on the proceeds of a bond issued at 43 cents on the dollar.

indicators.name: Warrant Valuation on Balance Sheet
indicators.definition: The market value assigned to warrants when they are used as part of acquisition consideration, reflected in the common-stock capital figure on the consolidated balance sheet.

indicators.name: EPS
indicators.definition: 每股收益（Earnings Per Share），衡量公司每股盈利能力。Graham指出样本中仅6家科技公司每股收益超过1美元

indicators.name: Dividend
indicators.definition: 股息支付情况，衡量公司是否向股东分配利润。样本中仅5家科技公司支付股息

indicators.name: Price Change
indicators.definition: 股价变动幅度，用于追踪投资后的表现。样本中超过半数公司股价跌幅超过50%

indicators.name: Profit/Loss Status
indicators.definition: 公司盈亏状态，46家科技公司中26家报告亏损，反映行业整体盈利质量低下

indicators.name: Letter Stock
indicators.definition: Letter stock是指未在美国证券交易委员会（SEC）注册出售的股票，购买者需提供一封信函声明该购买行为是出于投资目的。这类股票通常流动性较低，因为未经注册不能在公开市场上自由交易。

indicators.name: AAA Bond Yields
indicators.definition: Moody's AAA级债券收益率，代表最高信用评级债券的收益水平，常用作无风险或低风险收益率基准

indicators.name: Industrial Stock Returns
indicators.definition: 工业类股票的收益率或回报数据，用于与债券收益率进行比较分析

indicators.name: 债券与股票配置比例
indicators.definition: 投资组合中债券和股票的分配比例，用于控制整体风险水平，建议股票占比在25%-75%之间浮动

indicators.name: 高等级债券收益率
indicators.definition: 高评级债券（如国债、投资级公司债）的到期收益率，作为投资回报的基准参考

indicators.name: 市场整体估值水平
indicators.definition: 衡量股票市场整体是被高估还是低估的指标，用于指导资产配置比例的调整

indicators.name: 股息税率 (Dividend Tax Rate)
indicators.definition: 适用于投资者所获股息收入的税率，此处假设最高税率档次为40%。

indicators.name: 资本利得税率 (Capital Gains Tax Rate)
indicators.definition: 适用于投资者因出售资产获得的资本利得的税率，此处假设为20%。

indicators.name: 税后回报率 (After-Tax Return)
indicators.definition: 扣除股息税和资本利得税后的实际投资回报率，是衡量投资者真实收益的关键指标。

indicators.name: Rate of Return on Asset Value (S&P 425 Industrials)
indicators.definition: The rate earned on the Standard & Poor's index of 425 industrial stocks measured against asset (book) value, reported as approximately 11%.

indicators.name: Earnings Growth Rate (盈利增长率)
indicators.definition: 企业或市场整体每股收益的年化增长率，用于衡量企业盈利能力的变化趋势。

indicators.name: Bond Yield (债券收益率)
indicators.definition: 高等级债券的到期收益率，作为与股票股息收益率对比的基准，用于判断股票相对于债券的吸引力。

indicators.name: DJIA (道琼斯工业平均指数)
indicators.definition: 美国30只大型工业股票的价格加权平均指数，用于反映美国股市整体走势和长期历史表现。

indicators.name: S&P 500 Composite Index
indicators.definition: 标准普尔500指数，涵盖美国500家大型上市公司，是衡量美国股市整体表现的重要基准。

indicators.name: S&P Utility Average
indicators.definition: Standard & Poor's编制的公用事业公司股票平均指数，用于追踪公用事业板块表现

indicators.name: S&P Transportation Average
indicators.definition: Standard & Poor's编制的交通运输（主要是铁路）公司股票平均指数

indicators.name: Dow Jones Utility Average
indicators.definition: 道琼斯编制的公用事业公司股票平均指数

indicators.name: Dow Jones Transportation Average
indicators.definition: 道琼斯编制的交通运输（主要是铁路）公司股票平均指数

indicators.name: NYSE Composite Index
indicators.definition: 纽约证券交易所自1965年起编制的综合指数，代表其所有上市普通股的整体走势

indicators.name: DJIA Median Valuation
indicators.definition: 基于研究得出的道琼斯工业平均指数的中位数估值，用于判断市场整体是否被高估或低估

indicators.name: Discounted Value
indicators.definition: 将未来预期估值折现到当前时点的价值，例如将1975年预期的DJIA 1520折现为1971年中期约1200的现值

indicators.name: DJIA (Dow Jones Industrial Average)
indicators.definition: 道琼斯工业平均指数，衡量美国30家大型上市公司股价表现的价格加权指数

indicators.name: Tax-free yield
indicators.definition: 债券的免税收益率，即投资者从免税债券中获得的不需缴纳所得税的收益率

indicators.name: Volatility
indicators.definition: Average price variations over time, commonly used in modern mathematical finance as a measure of risk, but criticized by Graham as an inadequate and potentially misleading definition of true investment risk.

indicators.name: Bond Credit Rating
indicators.definition: A rating assigned by agencies (e.g., Moody's, S&P) indicating the creditworthiness of a bond issuer and the likelihood of default. Investment-grade is BBB/Baa or above.

indicators.name: Yield Spread
indicators.definition: The difference in yield between a lower-grade bond and a high-grade or government bond, representing the additional compensation for taking on higher credit risk.

indicators.name: Price-to-Par Ratio
indicators.definition: The current market price of a bond or preferred stock relative to its par (face) value, used to identify bargain opportunities in distressed securities.

indicators.name: Coupon Rate
indicators.definition: The annual interest rate paid by a bond on its face value, relevant for assessing whether a discounted bond offers sufficient yield.

indicators.name: Net Income / Deficit
indicators.definition: 公司净利润或净亏损，反映公司整体盈利能力，大额亏损（deficit）是财务困境的重要信号

indicators.name: Bond Price
indicators.definition: 债券的市场交易价格，面值通常为100，价格跌至10意味着债券价值仅为面值的10%，反映市场对发行人偿债能力的极度悲观预期

indicators.name: Interest Coverage
indicators.definition: 公司盈利对债券利息的覆盖能力，当公司无法覆盖利息支出时可能暂停支付

indicators.name: 累积未付股息（Accumulated Dividends）
indicators.definition: 优先股应付但尚未支付的累积股息总额，反映公司对优先股股东的欠付义务

indicators.name: 优先股市场价格与累积股息比率
indicators.definition: 优先股当前市场价格与其累积未付股息之间的比率，比率越低说明被低估程度越大

indicators.name: 优先股票面价值/清算价值
indicators.definition: 优先股在退市、赎回或交换时公司承诺支付的价值，如本例中每股兑换$196.50的债券

indicators.name: 价格跌幅（从高点回撤）
indicators.definition: 股票从最高价下跌的百分比，用于衡量投机性股票的下行风险

indicators.name: Long-term Debt to Net Current Assets
indicators.definition: Ratio of long-term debt to working capital (current assets minus current liabilities). Debt should not exceed net current assets.

indicators.name: Earnings Stability (5-year)
indicators.definition: Whether the company has reported positive earnings in each of the past five years with no deficit.

indicators.name: Earnings Growth (5-year)
indicators.definition: Comparison of current earnings to earnings five years ago; current should exceed the earlier figure.

indicators.name: Price-to-Earnings Ratio (P/E)
indicators.definition: Stock price divided by average earnings per share over the last three years. Should not exceed 15.

indicators.name: Price-to-Book Ratio (P/B)
indicators.definition: Stock price relative to net tangible asset value per share. Price should not exceed 120% of book value.

indicators.name: Working Capital per Share
indicators.definition: Net current assets (current assets minus current liabilities) per share; used to identify deeply undervalued stocks.

indicators.name: EPS年均增长率（5年）
indicators.definition: 过去至少连续五年的每股净利润年均复合增长率，用于判断公司是否真正属于成长股

indicators.name: 每股净利润（EPS）
indicators.definition: 公司净利润除以总股本，衡量公司盈利能力的核心指标

indicators.name: Dow Theory Buy/Sell Signals
indicators.definition: 道氏理论通过道琼斯工业平均指数和运输平均指数的相互确认来产生买入和卖出信号，用于判断市场主要趋势的方向

indicators.name: 债券信用评级
indicators.definition: 由Moody's和Standard & Poor's等评级机构对债券和优先股的信用质量进行评估，Moody's使用Aaa到D的评级体系，S&P使用AAA到D的评级体系，前三个等级代表投资级别中最高的信用质量

indicators.name: 债券收益率/利率
indicators.definition: 债券的票面利率或到期收益率，如文中提到的浮动利率贷款票据和7%-8%的债券，反映债券的收益水平和市场利率环境

indicators.name: Sales Charge (销售费用/申购费)
indicators.definition: 购买基金时支付的前端费用，通常以销售价格（含费用）的百分比表示，但以净资产值为基数计算时实际费率更高。

indicators.name: NAV (净资产值)
indicators.definition: 基金每份额的净资产价值，即基金总资产减去总负债后除以总份额数，是衡量基金真实价值的基础指标。

indicators.name: Earnings Record
indicators.definition: A company's history of earnings over multiple years, used to assess stability and growth trends.

indicators.name: Dividend History
indicators.definition: The track record of dividend payments, indicating financial health and management's commitment to returning value to shareholders.

indicators.name: Balance Sheet Strength
indicators.definition: Assessment of a company's assets, liabilities, and net worth to determine financial soundness and solvency.

indicators.name: P/E Ratio (Price-to-Earnings)
indicators.definition: The ratio of a stock's current price to its earnings per share, used to gauge whether a stock is overvalued or undervalued.

indicators.name: Multiplier (P/E Ratio)
indicators.definition: 市盈率倍数，用于衡量股票价格相对于每股收益的倍数关系

indicators.name: Expected Growth Rate
indicators.definition: 预期增长率，指公司未来盈利或收入的预期增长速度

indicators.name: 股票拆分（Stock Split）
indicators.definition: 公司将现有股票按一定比例拆分为更多股份，如二拆一（two-for-one split），总市值不变但每股价格降低，可能短期刺激股价上涨

indicators.name: 管理层质量
indicators.definition: 评估公司管理层的能力和诚信度，包括其过往业绩记录、会计实践的合规性以及财务报表的真实性

indicators.name: Warrant Market Value
indicators.definition: 公司已发行权证的当前市场价值，用于衡量潜在的股权稀释规模。

indicators.name: Adjusted Market Capitalization
indicators.definition: 普通股总市值加上权证市场价值后的调整市值，更准确地反映公司的全部股权价值。

indicators.name: 债券收益率 (Bond Yield)
indicators.definition: 最高等级债券的年化收益率，如1972年初为7.19%

indicators.name: 股票股息收益率 (Stock Dividend Yield)
indicators.definition: 代表性普通股的股息收益率，如1972年初工业股为2.76%

indicators.name: 债券收益率与股票收益率之比
indicators.definition: 高等级债券收益率与股票股息收益率的比值，用于判断两类资产的相对吸引力

indicators.name: 行业收入增长率
indicators.definition: 行业收入的增长速度，但Graham警告高收入增长不等于高利润或高投资回报

indicators.name: DJIA Average Price Per Share
indicators.definition: 道琼斯工业平均指数成分股经过多次拆股等调整后的实际平均每股价格，1972年初约为53美元。

indicators.name: Current Ratio（流动比率）
indicators.definition: 流动资产除以流动负债，衡量公司短期偿债能力。Graham要求该比率至少为2。

indicators.name: Net Current Assets（净流动资产）
indicators.definition: 流动资产减去流动负债，即营运资本。Graham要求净流动资产应超过公司总债务。

indicators.name: Total Debt（总债务）
indicators.definition: 公司的长期和短期债务总额，用于与净流动资产进行比较以评估财务安全性。

indicators.name: 债务水平/债券负担
indicators.definition: 企业发行债券的规模及负债程度，heavily bonded 表示企业大量依赖债券融资，负债比率较高

indicators.name: 购买合同保障
indicators.definition: 企业底层购买合同（purchase contracts）对债券偿付的担保能力，用于评估债务安全性

indicators.name: S&P 500 Composite
indicators.definition: 标准普尔500指数，由500只大型股票组成的市场基准指数，用于衡量基金业绩表现的参考基准

indicators.name: DJIA
indicators.definition: 道琼斯工业平均指数，由30只大型工业股票组成的价格加权指数，作为市场基准之一

indicators.name: 债券到期收益率 (Bond Yield)
indicators.definition: 债券持有至到期的年化收益率，用于衡量债券投资的回报水平

indicators.name: 转换溢价/收益牺牲比例 (Yield Sacrifice for Conversion Privilege)
indicators.definition: 投资者为获得可转换权利而放弃的收益占非可转换同类债券收益的百分比，本例中为40%（从7.5%降至4.5%）

indicators.name: 债券信用优先级 (Bond Seniority/Subordination)
indicators.definition: 债券在发行人破产清算时的求偿顺序，次级债券（subordinated）的求偿权低于优先债券

indicators.name: 转换价格 (Conversion Price)
indicators.definition: 可转换债券持有人将债券转换为股票时的参考股价，本例中为福特汽车当时股价68美元

indicators.name: 优先股与普通股价差（Preferred-Common Spread）
indicators.definition: 同一公司优先股价格与普通股价格之间的差额，用于评估两者之间的相对价值和套利机会

indicators.name: 优先股股息率（Preferred Dividend Yield）
indicators.definition: 优先股的固定股息与其市场价格的比率，用于与普通股股息进行比较以评估相对吸引力

indicators.name: 基准利率/联邦基金利率
indicators.definition: 央行设定的基准利率水平，反映整体利率环境，利率下降有利于股票市场

indicators.name: 国债收益率
indicators.definition: 政府债券的到期收益率，作为无风险利率的代表，用于衡量固定收益资产的回报水平

indicators.name: 股票风险溢价（Equity Risk Premium）
indicators.definition: 股票预期回报率与无风险利率之间的差值，利率越低，股票相对吸引力越大

indicators.name: 股息率（Dividend Yield）
indicators.definition: 每股股息与股价的比率，在低利率环境下，高股息率的股票对收益型投资者尤其有吸引力

indicators.name: 利润率/盈利能力
indicators.definition: 公司的盈利水平，高回报率会向市场发出信号，吸引新竞争者进入该行业

indicators.name: Undistributed Earnings
indicators.definition: 未分配利润，即公司净利润中未以股息形式支付而留存在公司内部的部分。

indicators.name: Fixed Charge Coverage Ratio
indicators.definition: 企业盈利与固定费用（利息等）的比率，用于衡量债券和优先股的安全边际

indicators.name: Annual Earnings Multipliers
indicators.definition: Price-to-earnings ratios used to value stocks, discussed in context of security analysis (e.g., pp. 295-97)

indicators.name: Per-Share Earnings
indicators.definition: Earnings allocated to each share of common stock, subject to various accounting adjustments and practices

indicators.name: Book Value (Asset Backing)
indicators.definition: Net asset value per share as shown on the balance sheet, used in stock selection for aggressive investors

indicators.name: Dividends
indicators.definition: Cash distributions to shareholders, relevant to both defensive and aggressive investor stock selection criteria

indicators.name: Per-share earnings
indicators.definition: Net income divided by shares outstanding, with attention to dilution from convertible issues and warrants

indicators.name: Earning power
indicators.definition: A company's demonstrated ability to generate earnings over time, used as a key component in margin of safety analysis

indicators.name: Credit ratings
indicators.definition: Ratings assigned to bonds and debt instruments indicating creditworthiness and default risk

indicators.name: Earnings-covered test
indicators.definition: A security analysis test measuring whether a company's earnings sufficiently cover its debt obligations and interest payments

indicators.name: Margin of safety
indicators.definition: The difference between a security's intrinsic value and its market price, providing a buffer against errors in analysis or market downturns

indicators.name: Payout ratio
indicators.definition: The proportion of earnings paid out as dividends to shareholders

indicators.name: Consumer Price Index
indicators.definition: A measure of inflation tracking changes in the price level of a basket of consumer goods and services

indicators.name: Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA)
indicators.definition: A stock market index of 30 large publicly traded U.S. companies, used as a benchmark for market performance and stock selection

indicators.name: Dollar-cost averaging
indicators.definition: An investment strategy of investing fixed amounts at regular intervals regardless of market price to reduce the impact of volatility

indicators.name: Net Current Asset Value
indicators.definition: Current assets minus total liabilities; stocks trading below this value are considered bargain issues in the Graham-Newman method

indicators.name: Book Value (Net Asset Value)
indicators.definition: 净资产价值，公司资产减去负债后的价值，用于评估股票是否被低估

indicators.name: Fund Expense Ratio
indicators.definition: 基金费用率，衡量共同基金运营成本占资产的比例，直接影响投资者的净回报

indicators.name: Management Compensation
indicators.definition: 管理层薪酬，包括工资、奖金和股票期权，过高的薪酬可能损害股东利益

indicators.name: Price-to-Book Value Ratio
indicators.definition: The ratio of a stock's market price to its book value per share, used to assess whether a stock is undervalued or overvalued.

indicators.name: Price-to-Assets Ratio
indicators.definition: The ratio of a stock's market price to its net asset value per share.

indicators.name: ROIC (Return on Invested Capital)
indicators.definition: A measure of how effectively a company uses its invested capital to generate returns; used to evaluate profitability and management efficiency.

indicators.name: Bond Rating
indicators.definition: A credit quality assessment of bonds issued by rating agencies, used to evaluate default risk.

indicators.name: Tangible-asset value (Book Value)
indicators.definition: The net asset value of a company based on tangible assets, used as a baseline for valuation and margin of safety analysis

indicators.name: Price/Earnings Ratio
indicators.definition: Ratio of stock price to per-share earnings, central to the two-part appraisal process for valuation

indicators.name: Per-share Earnings
indicators.definition: Net income divided by shares outstanding; subject to adjustments for taxes, dilution, and special charges to assess dependability of value

indicators.name: Safety of Principal
indicators.definition: The degree to which an investment operation protects the original capital from loss, as determined by thorough analysis.

indicators.name: Adequate Return
indicators.definition: A satisfactory return on investment that is commensurate with the level of risk taken, distinguishing investment from speculation.

indicators.name: Market Sentiment / Public Opinion
indicators.definition: The prevailing attitude of the general public toward stocks (e.g., percentage viewing stocks as safe vs. a gamble), which can serve as a contrarian indicator of market valuation attractiveness.

indicators.name: 股息收益率 (Dividend Yield)
indicators.definition: 股票每年支付的股息与其市场价格的比率，用于衡量股票的收入回报

indicators.name: 股票预期总回报率
indicators.definition: 股息收益率加上内在价值的稳定增长率，格雷厄姆估计在正常市场水平约为7.5%/年

indicators.name: 资产配置比例
indicators.definition: 投资组合中债券与股票的分配比例，用于控制风险和回报的平衡

indicators.name: 债券收益率
indicators.definition: 优质公司债券的可获得回报率，1971年左右约为7.5%，反映市场利率水平

indicators.name: 股息收益率（DJIA型股票）
indicators.definition: 道琼斯工业平均指数成分股的股息回报率，1964年末约为3.2%，1971年（道指900点时）低于3.5%

indicators.name: 利率变动对债券价格的影响
indicators.definition: 利率上升导致中期（约20年期）债券市场价格最大下跌约38%

indicators.name: Expected Stock Total Return (股票预期总回报)
indicators.definition: 股息收益率加上预期年均资本增值率（基于公司未分配利润再投资），即Gordon方程：未来回报=当前股息率+预期盈利增长率

indicators.name: After-tax Return (税后回报率)
indicators.definition: 考虑税收影响后的实际投资回报率，1971年末股票税后约5.3%，与免税中期债券相当

indicators.name: Inflation Rate (通胀率)
indicators.definition: 消费者价格指数的年度变化率，会侵蚀投资的实际回报

indicators.name: EPS Growth
indicators.definition: 每股收益增长率，反映公司盈利的增长趋势

indicators.name: Expected Earnings Growth
indicators.definition: 预期盈利增长，基于分析师预测或公司指引的未来盈利变化

indicators.name: 净流动资产价值（Net Current Asset Value / NCAV）
indicators.definition: 流动资产减去所有负债（包括优先于普通股的所有负债），不计算固定资产和其他长期资产。当股价低于每股净流动资产价值时，表明股票被严重低估

indicators.name: 营运资本（Working Capital）
indicators.definition: 流动资产减去流动负债，用于衡量企业短期偿债能力和流动性

indicators.name: 过去增长记录（Past Growth Record）
indicators.definition: 公司历史盈利增长表现，常被用作预测未来增长的依据，但格雷厄姆警告这种外推可能不可靠

indicators.name: 盈利能力（Earning Power）
indicators.definition: 公司预期的长期盈利能力，尤其用于评估尚未展现出色业绩但预期未来将建立高盈利能力的公司

indicators.name: 公司基本业务价值
indicators.definition: 公司底层业务的内在价值，区别于股票市场价格，用于判断股价下跌是否反映了真实的价值变化

indicators.name: 一月效应（January Effect）
indicators.definition: 小盘股在年末（12月下旬）到次年1月初期间倾向于产生较大涨幅的季节性现象。1962-1979年超额收益约8.5个百分点，1980-1989年约4.4个百分点，1990-2001年约5.8个百分点，但交易成本可高达8%

indicators.name: 交易成本
indicators.definition: 买卖极小盘股的总交易成本，据Plexus Group统计可高达投资额的8%，足以吞噬日历效应等策略的超额收益

indicators.name: Dividend Yield（股息收益率）
indicators.definition: 每股年度股息除以当前股价，衡量投资者每投入一元所获得的股息回报比例

indicators.name: Stock Price（股价）
indicators.definition: 股票的当前市场交易价格，在此策略中用于在高股息股票中进一步筛选低价股

indicators.name: January Effect
indicators.definition: Historical anomaly where stocks (especially small caps) tend to outperform in January; cited as an example of a real pattern whose returns diminished once widely publicized

indicators.name: Consumer Price Index (CPI)
indicators.definition: 消费者价格指数，衡量一般消费品和服务价格水平的变化，用于衡量通货膨胀率

indicators.name: Wholesale Price Index
indicators.definition: 批发价格指数，衡量批发层面商品价格水平的变化

indicators.name: S&P 500 Stock Index Price
indicators.definition: 标准普尔500指数的价格水平，反映美国大盘股的整体市场价值

indicators.name: S&P 500 Earnings
indicators.definition: 标准普尔500指数成分股的每股收益，反映企业盈利能力

indicators.name: Inflation Rate
indicators.definition: 通货膨胀率，衡量货币购买力下降的速度，通常以CPI年度变化率表示

indicators.name: Stock Price Level
indicators.definition: 股票价格的整体水平，可通过标准普尔500综合指数或道琼斯工业平均指数衡量

indicators.name: Bear/Bull Market Cycle Decline Percentage
indicators.definition: 熊市与牛市周期中从高点到低点的跌幅百分比，用于衡量市场波动的严重程度

indicators.name: P/E Ratio (Price/Earnings Ratio)
indicators.definition: 股票价格除以每股收益。低于10为低估，10-20为适中，大于20为昂贵。用于衡量市场整体估值水平

indicators.name: Payout Ratio
indicators.definition: 股息占盈利的百分比，反映公司将多少利润分配给股东。历史数据显示该比率在50%-80%之间波动

indicators.name: Dividend Growth Rate
indicators.definition: 股息的年均增长率，反映公司分红能力的长期趋势

indicators.name: Annual Compounded Rate of Price Advance
indicators.definition: 股价的年均复合增长率。1949-1966年DJIA年均复合增长率约11%（不含约3.5%的股息）

indicators.name: Rate of Profit on Capital
indicators.definition: 资本利润率，衡量企业整体盈利能力。1970年该指标出现明显恶化，降至二战以来最低水平

indicators.name: Price/Earnings Ratio (3-year average)
indicators.definition: The ratio of the current market price to the average earnings per share over the past three calendar years, used to smooth out earnings fluctuations and assess market valuation

indicators.name: Price/Last Year's Earnings
indicators.definition: The ratio of the current market price to the most recent year's earnings per share

indicators.name: High-Grade Bond Interest Rate
indicators.definition: The yield on S&P AAA-rated bonds, used as a benchmark for comparing stock market attractiveness

indicators.name: Dividend Yield / Bond Yield Ratio
indicators.definition: The ratio of the stock market's dividend yield to the high-grade bond yield, indicating the relative attractiveness of stocks versus bonds

indicators.name: Stock Return / Bond Return Ratio
indicators.definition: 股票收益率（盈利/价格）与债券收益率的比率，用于判断股票相对于债券的吸引力

indicators.name: Bond Yield / Stock Dividend Yield Ratio
indicators.definition: 债券收益率与股票股息收益率的比率，用于评估两类资产的相对价值

indicators.name: NASDAQ Composite Index
indicators.definition: 纳斯达克综合指数，涵盖纳斯达克交易所上市的所有股票的市值加权指数

indicators.name: PE (市盈率)
indicators.definition: 股票价格与每股收益的比率，用于衡量股票估值水平；文中提到某些股票以'无限市盈率'交易，表明极度高估

indicators.name: 历史实际回报率（扣除通胀后）
indicators.definition: 投资在扣除通货膨胀后的真实年化回报率，用于比较股票、债券和现金的长期表现

indicators.name: 幸存者偏差 (Survivorship Bias)
indicators.definition: 历史股票指数忽略了已破产退市公司的损失，导致历史回报率被高估的统计偏差

indicators.name: Stock-Bond Allocation Ratio
indicators.definition: 投资组合中普通股与债券的配置比例，用于衡量整体资产配置的风险水平

indicators.name: DJIA Dividend Yield
indicators.definition: 道琼斯工业平均指数的股息收益率，即每单位指数的年度股息除以指数价格

indicators.name: Dividend Yield to Bond Yield Ratio
indicators.definition: 股票股息收益率与债券收益率的比值，当该比值达到约2/3时可作为增加股票配置的信号

indicators.name: Bond Safety
indicators.definition: The degree of certainty that the bond issuer will meet all interest and principal payment obligations.

indicators.name: Income-Tax Status
indicators.definition: The tax treatment of bond income, which affects the after-tax return to the investor.

indicators.name: 到期收益率 (Yield to Maturity)
indicators.definition: 债券持有至到期时的年化收益率。E系列债券持有至到期（5年10个月）的收益率为5%，半年复利计算

indicators.name: 当前赎回价值 (Current Redemption Value)
indicators.definition: 债券在到期前任意时点可赎回的价值，E系列债券的赎回价值随持有时间增长而增加

indicators.name: 税后有效收益率 (Effective Net-After-Tax Rate)
indicators.definition: 考虑税收递延优势后的实际收益率，储蓄债券因可递延联邦所得税，税后有效收益率可比名义收益率高出约三分之一

indicators.name: 票面利率 (Coupon Rate)
indicators.definition: H系列债券第一年利率为4.29%，之后9年为5.10%的固定利率

indicators.name: 债券到期收益率 (Yield to Maturity)
indicators.definition: 债券持有至到期日所获得的年化收益率，包括票息收入和资本利得/损失

indicators.name: 债券折价率 (Bond Discount)
indicators.definition: 债券市场价格低于面值的幅度，如面值100的债券以60交易则折价40%

indicators.name: 收益率利差 (Yield Spread)
indicators.definition: 间接政府义务债券与同期限直接政府债券之间的收益率差异

indicators.name: 债券期限分类
indicators.definition: 长期（超过10年）、中期（3-5年）、短期的分类，不同期限对应不同收益率和风险特征

indicators.name: Maturity
indicators.definition: 债券的到期期限，期限越长通常收益率越高，反映期限溢价

indicators.name: 税后净收益率 (Net After-Tax Yield)
indicators.definition: 扣除联邦和州税后的实际债券收益率，用于比较免税债券和应税债券的实际回报

indicators.name: 赎回溢价 (Call Premium)
indicators.definition: 发行方提前赎回债券时支付的高于发行价的溢价，通常约为发行价的5%

indicators.name: 债券期限 (Maturity)
indicators.definition: 债券到期的时间长度，短期债券收益率通常低于长期债券

indicators.name: 债券到期收益率（Yield to Maturity）
indicators.definition: 债券从购买到到期日的年化收益率，考虑票息和价格折溢价

indicators.name: 票息率（Coupon Rate）
indicators.definition: 债券的固定利率，即债券面值上支付的年利息百分比

indicators.name: 赎回保护期（Call Protection Period）
indicators.definition: 债券发行后不可被发行方提前赎回的年限

indicators.name: 优先股股息率（Preferred Dividend Rate）
indicators.definition: 优先股的固定股息率，必须在普通股股息之前支付

indicators.name: 债券折价/溢价
indicators.definition: 债券市场价格相对于面值的折扣或溢价程度

indicators.name: Bond Interest Rate / Bond Yield
indicators.definition: The yield on good bonds (e.g., long-term Treasury bonds), used as a comparison benchmark against common stock dividend yields.

indicators.name: portfolio_stock_count
indicators.definition: 投资组合中持有的不同股票（证券）的数量，用于衡量投资组合的分散化程度。

indicators.name: Prominence / Brand Recognition
indicators.definition: 公司在行业中的知名度和地位，通常可通过是否为行业龙头、是否属于主要指数成分股等来衡量

indicators.name: 连续股息支付记录
indicators.definition: 公司连续支付股息的年数，用于衡量公司财务稳定性和对股东回报的承诺

indicators.name: 股息收益率
indicators.definition: 每股股息与股价的比率，文中提到股票收益率与债券收益率的历史关系发生了根本性变化

indicators.name: 连续股息支付年数
indicators.definition: 公司连续不间断支付现金股息的年数，用于衡量公司盈利稳定性和对股东回报的承诺

indicators.name: PE (基于7年平均收益)
indicators.definition: 股票当前价格与过去七年平均每股收益的比率，用于衡量长期估值水平

indicators.name: PE (基于最近12个月收益)
indicators.definition: 股票当前价格与最近十二个月每股收益的比率，即滚动市盈率

indicators.name: 每股收益增长率
indicators.definition: 每股收益的年复合增长率，成长股通常被定义为十年内每股收益翻倍，即年复合增长率超过7.1%

indicators.name: 收益倍数 (Earnings Multiplier)
indicators.definition: 投资者愿意为每单位公司盈利支付的价格，本质上等同于市盈率，用于衡量估值合理性

indicators.name: Business Value (内在价值估算)
indicators.definition: 通过分析公司财务报表来估算公司的商业价值，作为判断股票是否被高估或低估的依据

indicators.name: Portfolio Concentration (持仓集中度)
indicators.definition: 衡量投资组合中单只股票或单一行业的权重占比，用于评估分散化程度

indicators.name: Trading Frequency (交易频率)
indicators.definition: 衡量投资者每年的交易次数，过高的交易频率通常意味着投机而非投资

indicators.name: Bond Credit Rating / Grade
indicators.definition: 债券的信用等级分类（一级/高等级 vs 二级/低等级），反映发行人的信用质量和违约风险

indicators.name: Bond Discount to Par
indicators.definition: 债券当前市场价格相对于面值（100）的折扣幅度，折扣越大可能提供本金增值机会

indicators.name: 政府担保状态
indicators.definition: 债券是否由美国政府有效担保，降低信用风险

indicators.name: 债券价格相对面值折扣
indicators.definition: 债券市场价格相对于面值的折扣程度，极低价格可能构成便宜货机会

indicators.name: 税收状态
indicators.definition: 债券利息收入是否免税，影响投资者的税后实际收益

indicators.name: PE_7yr_avg（七年平均市盈率）
indicators.definition: 股票价格除以过去七年的平均每股收益，用于平滑短期盈利波动，避免高估公司价值

indicators.name: 盈利增长率
indicators.definition: 公司收益随时间增长的速率，用于识别成长股，但Graham警告过去的高增长不能保证未来持续

indicators.name: 股票/债券配置比例
indicators.definition: 投资组合中普通股与债券的分配比例，Graham建议在25%-75%之间动态调整

indicators.name: Price-to-Earnings Ratio (PE)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股收益的比率，用于衡量股票相对于盈利的估值水平

indicators.name: Price-to-Book Ratio (PB)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股净资产的比率，用于判断股票是否低于资产价值

indicators.name: Reinvested Earnings Relative to Price
indicators.definition: 留存收益相对于买入价格的比率，衡量未分配利润对长期价值增长的贡献

indicators.name: Market Capitalization / Company Size
indicators.definition: 公司市值或规模，用于区分行业龙头与二线公司

indicators.name: Bond Discount Rate
indicators.definition: 债券市场价格相对于面值的折价幅度，用于识别廉价债券机会

indicators.name: Fair Value (公允价值)
indicators.definition: 基于基本面分析得出的股票内在价值，用于判断当前市场价格是否被高估或低估

indicators.name: Dow Theory (道氏理论)
indicators.definition: 一种技术分析方法，通过股票指数的特定突破信号来判断买入和卖出时机，在上行突破时发出买入信号，在下行突破时发出卖出信号

indicators.name: Market Price vs. Intrinsic Value
indicators.definition: 将股票的市场报价与其基本面所支持的内在价值进行比较，作为买卖决策的依据

indicators.name: Dividend Income
indicators.definition: 股息收入，投资者在等待市场时机时可能损失的收益，需要在择时决策中考虑其机会成本

indicators.name: Tangible Book Value (Tangible-Asset Value)
indicators.definition: The value of a company's assets as shown on the balance sheet minus intangible assets and liabilities, providing a justification or support independent of fluctuating market prices

indicators.name: Price-to-Book Ratio (Price / Tangible Book Value)
indicators.definition: The ratio of a stock's market price to its tangible book value per share. The premium over book value can be considered as an extra fee paid for stock-exchange listing and marketability.

indicators.name: Earnings-to-Price Ratio
indicators.definition: The ratio of a company's earnings per share to its stock price, indicating the return on investment at the current price

indicators.name: Net Current Assets (Working Capital)
indicators.definition: Current assets minus all liabilities; when a company's market cap is below this value, it is selling for less than its liquidation value

indicators.name: Five-Year Average Earnings
indicators.definition: The average of a company's annual earnings over the past five years, used to smooth out cyclical fluctuations and provide a more stable basis for valuation

indicators.name: Net Asset Value (NAV)
indicators.definition: 基金每份额的净资产价值，等于基金总资产减去总负债后除以总份额数，是开放式基金赎回和申购的基准价格

indicators.name: Sales Load (销售费用)
indicators.definition: 购买基金时收取的销售佣金，通常约为资产价值的5.75%（历史上曾高达9%），直接减少投资者的初始投资金额

indicators.name: Fund Expense Ratio (基金费用率)
indicators.definition: 基金运营管理费用占基金资产的比例，包括投资顾问费等，免佣基金通常仅收取常规投资顾问费

indicators.name: Portfolio Composition (投资组合构成)
indicators.definition: 基金持有的资产类别比例，平衡型基金通常约三分之一为债券，股票型基金几乎全部为普通股

indicators.name: Fund Total Return (multi-year)
indicators.definition: The overall percentage return of a mutual fund over a specified period (e.g., 5 years or 10 years), including capital gains and income distributions.

indicators.name: S&P 500 Composite Index Return
indicators.definition: The total return of the Standard & Poor's 500-stock composite index over a given period, used as a benchmark for fund performance comparison.

indicators.name: DJIA Return
indicators.definition: The total return of the Dow Jones Industrial Average over a given period; Graham notes the DJIA can diverge from the S&P 500 over 10-year periods but converges over 25-50 year horizons.

indicators.name: Fund Load
indicators.definition: The sales charge or commission paid when purchasing mutual fund shares. Load funds charge a fee; no-load funds do not.

indicators.name: Fund Net Assets
indicators.definition: The total net asset value of a mutual fund, indicating its size. Larger funds may be more representative of overall industry performance.

indicators.name: 基金资产净值折价/溢价率
indicators.definition: 封闭式基金市场价格相对于其每股资产净值的折价或溢价百分比，折价意味着市场价格低于资产净值

indicators.name: 基金规模（总资产）
indicators.definition: 基金管理的总资产规模，较小规模的基金可能更容易获得持续优异的投资回报

indicators.name: S&P综合指数
indicators.definition: 标准普尔综合指数，作为衡量基金经理业绩表现的基准指标

indicators.name: 基金年度回报率
indicators.definition: 基金在特定年度的投资回报率，用于评估基金经理的投资能力和基金的长期表现

indicators.name: Principal Value Conservation
indicators.definition: The preservation of the original invested capital over time, the primary goal of conservative investment counsel

indicators.name: Dividend Yield / Income Rate
indicators.definition: The conservatively acceptable rate of income produced by standard interest- and dividend-paying securities

indicators.name: Portfolio Allocation to Non-Leading Securities
indicators.definition: The percentage of total fund invested in securities other than leading companies and government bonds; should typically not exceed 10% for conservative investors

indicators.name: Interest Coverage Ratio (利息覆盖倍数)
indicators.definition: 可用收益与总利息费用的比率，用于衡量企业偿付债券利息的能力，通常取多年平均值

indicators.name: Preferred Dividend Coverage (优先股股息覆盖倍数)
indicators.definition: 可用收益与（债券利息+优先股股息）合计的比率，用于衡量优先股的安全性

indicators.name: Earnings Multiplier (市盈率/收益乘数)
indicators.definition: 股价与每股收益的比率，高乘数的成长股估值更多依赖于未来预测，风险更大

indicators.name: Growth Rate (盈利增长率)
indicators.definition: 公司盈利的年化增长速度，是成长股估值的关键变量

indicators.name: Capital Structure (资本结构)
indicators.definition: 公司债务与股权的构成比例，影响公司财务安全性

indicators.name: Working Capital (营运资本)
indicators.definition: 流动资产减去流动负债，反映公司短期偿债能力和流动性

indicators.name: Asset Values (资产价值)
indicators.definition: 公司资产的账面价值或评估价值，是评估证券安全性的重要因素之一

indicators.name: 利息覆盖倍数（税前）
indicators.definition: 税前利润与总固定费用（利息支出等）的比率，衡量公司偿付利息的能力

indicators.name: 利息覆盖倍数（税后）
indicators.definition: 税后利润与总固定费用的比率，从税后角度衡量偿债能力

indicators.name: 优先股覆盖倍数
indicators.definition: 税前利润与（固定费用+两倍优先股股息）的比率，两倍优先股股息是为了调整优先股股息不可税前扣除的影响

indicators.name: 税前盈利占债务本金比例
indicators.definition: 税前盈利除以债务本金总额的百分比，作为利率上升环境下利息覆盖倍数的替代指标

indicators.name: 7年平均盈利覆盖率
indicators.definition: 过去7年盈利覆盖率的平均值，用于平滑经济周期波动，评估长期偿债能力

indicators.name: 最差年份盈利覆盖率
indicators.definition: 过去7年中盈利覆盖率最低的年份数值，作为7年平均值的替代测试标准

indicators.name: 营业收入/业务量（Volume of Business）
indicators.definition: 企业的营业收入或业务规模，用于衡量企业是否达到投资所需的最低规模标准

indicators.name: 企业规模（Size of Enterprise）
indicators.definition: 根据企业所属行业（工业、公用事业、铁路）设定不同的最低规模门槛，作为筛选投资标的的基本条件

indicators.name: 市政人口规模（Municipal Population）
indicators.definition: 对于市政债券，所在城市或地区的人口数量需达到最低标准，以确保税基和偿债能力

indicators.name: Stock/Equity Ratio
indicators.definition: 初级股票（普通股）的市场价格总额与债务总面值（或债务加优先股面值）的比率，用于粗略衡量高级证券（债券、优先股）所获得的保护程度或'缓冲垫'大小。该比率隐含了市场对企业未来前景的评估。

indicators.name: Property/Asset Value (Book Value)
indicators.definition: 资产负债表上显示或经评估的资产价值，在公用事业、房地产和投资公司中作为债券和优先股安全性的独立测试

indicators.name: Interest Payments to Profits Ratio
indicators.definition: 利息支付占税前利润（含利息）的比例，用于衡量公司债务负担程度。1963年为16%，1971年升至29%，表明负担加重

indicators.name: Capitalization Factor (Multiplier)
indicators.definition: 用于将预估未来盈利转化为内在价值的乘数，基于公司历史关系和增长前景确定

indicators.name: Capitalization Structure
indicators.definition: 公司的资本结构，包括债务与权益的比例，反映公司财务健康程度和过度举债风险

indicators.name: P/E Ratio
indicators.definition: 市盈率，即股票收盘价格与每股收益的比率，反映市场对公司未来盈利增长前景的预期。高市盈率意味着市场对该公司或行业前景更为乐观。

indicators.name: P/E Ratio Differential (行业间市盈率差异)
indicators.definition: 不同行业或公司之间市盈率的差异，反映市场对不同行业长期前景的相对信心程度

indicators.name: 管理层competence（管理层能力）
indicators.definition: 对公司管理层经营能力的评估，目前缺乏客观、量化和可靠的测试方法，主要通过过去业绩记录、未来盈利预测和长期前景间接反映

indicators.name: 历史业绩记录
indicators.definition: 公司过去的经营和财务表现记录，是评估管理层能力的重要间接指标

indicators.name: Surplus Cash
indicators.definition: 公司在满足运营需求后剩余的现金及现金等价物，反映公司的财务灵活性和安全边际

indicators.name: Capital Structure / Leverage
indicators.definition: 公司普通股与债券及优先股之间的比例关系。头重脚轻的结构（普通股占比过低）意味着高杠杆，在有利条件下可能带来巨大投机利润，但也增加了风险

indicators.name: Senior Securities Ratio
indicators.definition: 债券和优先股等高级证券占公司总资本结构的比例，比例越高意味着普通股股东面临的风险越大

indicators.name: Bank Loans
indicators.definition: 公司从银行借入的贷款总额，大量银行贷款会削弱公司的财务实力

indicators.name: Quality Rating
indicators.definition: 公司的质量评级，连续派息记录是其中一个重要的评判因素

indicators.name: Expected Annual Growth Rate
indicators.definition: 预期未来7-10年的年均盈利增长率，用于Graham成长股估值公式

indicators.name: Implied Growth Rate
indicators.definition: 由当前市盈率通过Graham公式反推得出的市场隐含预期增长率，公式为 (PE - 8.5) / 2

indicators.name: Price-to-Dividend Ratio
indicators.definition: 股价与每股股息的比率，在标准股息政策下可作为市盈率的替代估值指标

indicators.name: Current (Normal) Earnings
indicators.definition: 公司当前正常化的每股收益，排除一次性或异常项目后的盈利水平

indicators.name: 长期盈利增长率
indicators.definition: 公司过去10年收入和净利润的年均增长率，税前约10%或税后6%-7%被认为是可持续的

indicators.name: 研发费用占净销售额比率 (R&D to Sales)
indicators.definition: 公司研发支出占净销售额的百分比，反映公司对未来增长的投入程度，不同行业标准不同

indicators.name: CEO薪酬
indicators.definition: 公司首席执行官的年度总薪酬，用于评估管理层是否以股东利益为重

indicators.name: 期权悬挂比率 (Option Overhang)
indicators.definition: 已发行的股票期权数量占流通股总数的比例，反映未来潜在的股权稀释程度

indicators.name: 股票期权重新定价
indicators.definition: 公司是否取消已有的低价值内部人士期权并以更有利的价格重新发行，这是公司治理不善的信号

indicators.name: 收入稳定性
indicators.definition: 过去10年收入和净利润增长的平稳程度，用于判断公司是否为稳健的'马拉松选手'而非短跑选手

indicators.name: Owner Earnings
indicators.definition: Net income plus amortization and depreciation, minus normal capital expenditures, minus stock option costs, minus nonrecurring/extraordinary charges, minus pension fund income. A measure popularized by Warren Buffett to better reflect actual cash generated by the business.

indicators.name: Cash from Operations
indicators.definition: Cash generated from the company's core business operations as reported in the statement of cash flows

indicators.name: Long-term Debt to Total Capital Ratio
indicators.definition: Long-term debt (including preferred stock) divided by total capital; measures the company's leverage and financial risk

indicators.name: Earnings to Fixed Charges Ratio (Interest Coverage)
indicators.definition: Ratio of company earnings to its fixed interest payment obligations; indicates whether the company can comfortably service its debt

indicators.name: Insider Trading Activity
indicators.definition: Pattern of buying or selling of company shares by senior executives and directors; repeated large insider sales are a warning sign

indicators.name: Owner Earnings Per Share Growth Rate
indicators.definition: The average annual growth rate of owner earnings per share over a 10-year period; a steady 6-7% or higher indicates a stable cash generator with good growth prospects

indicators.name: Dividend Policy
indicators.definition: Whether a company pays dividends to shareholders; burden of proof is on the company to justify retaining earnings rather than distributing them

indicators.name: Relative Performance vs Competitors
indicators.definition: How a company's business results and stock performance compare to its industry rivals in both good and bad market conditions

indicators.name: Share Buyback Timing
indicators.definition: Whether a company repurchases its own shares at or near record high prices versus at undervalued levels

indicators.name: Insider Selling Activity
indicators.definition: The extent to which company managers and executives are selling their own stock options, particularly concurrent with share buyback programs

indicators.name: Primary Earnings Per Share
indicators.definition: Earnings per share before special charges and without accounting for dilution from convertible securities or warrants.

indicators.name: Fully Diluted Earnings Per Share
indicators.definition: Earnings per share calculated assuming all convertible bonds, convertible preferred stock, and stock-purchase warrants are exercised, thus increasing the share count and typically reducing EPS.

indicators.name: Net Income After Special Charges
indicators.definition: Earnings after deducting one-time or non-recurring special charges, providing a more conservative view of actual profitability.

indicators.name: Dilution Factor
indicators.definition: The reduction in earnings per share that results from the potential conversion of convertible bonds, preferred stock, or exercise of stock warrants into common shares. In some cases can reduce apparent earnings by half or more.

indicators.name: 每股收益(EPS)
indicators.definition: 公司净利润除以流通股数，是衡量公司盈利能力的核心指标。需区分稀释后EPS和扣除特别费用前后的EPS

indicators.name: 特别费用/非经常性项目
indicators.definition: 公司声称为一次性或非经常性的费用和损失，包括关闭业务、合同损失、重组费用等。投资者需判断这些是否真正属于非经常性

indicators.name: 税收抵免(来自过去亏损)
indicators.definition: 公司因过去年度亏损而获得的未来税收减免，可能使未来年份的净利润被人为抬高

indicators.name: 损失准备金
indicators.definition: 公司为预期未来损失而提前计提的准备金，可能导致损失无法归属到实际发生的年份，从而扭曲各年度的真实盈利

indicators.name: 稀释每股收益（Diluted EPS）
indicators.definition: 考虑可转换证券和认股权证全部行权后的每股收益，反映潜在的股权稀释效应

indicators.name: 折旧方法（直线法 vs 加速折旧法）
indicators.definition: 直线法均匀分摊资产成本，加速折旧法前期多摊后期少摊；折旧方法的切换会显著影响报告盈利，如Trane Co.通过切换回直线法使每股收益增长近20%

indicators.name: 存货计价方法（FIFO vs LIFO）
indicators.definition: FIFO先进先出法和LIFO后进先出法对存货成本和利润的计算产生不同影响，尤其在通胀环境下差异显著

indicators.name: 平均盈利（Average Earnings）
indicators.definition: 多年盈利的算术平均值，用于平滑商业周期波动，比单一年度盈利更能反映公司真实盈利能力

indicators.name: 特殊费用（Special Charges）
indicators.definition: 一次性计入的非经常性费用，可能不会反映在每股收益中，需要投资者特别关注其对真实盈利的影响

indicators.name: 净经营亏损结转（Net Operating Loss Carryforward）
indicators.definition: 企业过去的经营亏损可在未来最长20年内结转，减少未来应税收入，从而提高税后净利润

indicators.name: Pro Forma盈利
indicators.definition: 不遵循GAAP准则的'假设性'财务报表所报告的盈利，可能扭曲真实盈利状况

indicators.name: P/B (Price/Book Value)
indicators.definition: Market price per share divided by book value per share; indicates how much investors pay relative to net asset value

indicators.name: Net Profit Margin (Net/Sales)
indicators.definition: Net income as a percentage of total sales revenue, measuring operational profitability

indicators.name: ROE (Net per share / Book Value)
indicators.definition: Return on equity measured as earnings per share divided by book value per share

indicators.name: Working Capital to Debt Ratio
indicators.definition: Net working capital (current assets minus current liabilities) divided by total bonds and preferred stock

indicators.name: Earnings Per Share (EPS) - Multi-period Average
indicators.definition: Average earnings per share calculated over 3-year periods (e.g., 1968-1970, 1963-1965, 1958-1960) to smooth out fluctuations

indicators.name: ROE (Return on Book Value)
indicators.definition: 账面价值回报率，衡量公司相对于股东权益的盈利能力

indicators.name: Operating Income to Sales Ratio
indicators.definition: 营业收入与销售额的比率，反映每美元销售额中的利润水平，是衡量公司比较优势或劣势的指标

indicators.name: Per-Share Earnings (EPS)
indicators.definition: Net earnings divided by the number of shares outstanding, used as the basis for calculating earnings stability over a ten-year period.

indicators.name: Price Multiplier
indicators.definition: 价格乘数，即市盈率的另一种表述，高乘数意味着市场对公司未来增长预期较高

indicators.name: ELTRA Growth
indicators.definition: ELTRA公司的盈利增长指标，书中作为低市盈率但高增长率的典型案例

indicators.name: 流动比率 (Current Ratio)
indicators.definition: 流动资产与流动负债的比率，标准值为2:1，用于衡量公司短期偿债能力

indicators.name: 每股净资产/账面价值 (Net per share/Book Value)
indicators.definition: 每股有形账面价值，用于衡量公司每股的实际资产价值

indicators.name: 稀释每股收益 (Diluted EPS)
indicators.definition: 将可转换优先股等潜在稀释证券视为已转换为普通股后计算的每股收益，反映最保守的盈利水平

indicators.name: 市值 (Market Value)
indicators.definition: 公司在市场上的总价值，用于评估公司规模和可转换证券的稀释影响

indicators.name: Dividend Continuity (股息连续性)
indicators.definition: 公司不间断支付股息的历史年数，反映公司长期盈利稳定性和对股东回报的承诺

indicators.name: Dividend Payout Ratio (股息支付比率)
indicators.definition: 公司将净利润中多大比例以股息形式分配给股东，即每股股息除以每股收益

indicators.name: Price History Range (High/Low Ratio)
indicators.definition: The ratio of the highest price to the lowest price over a long period (e.g., 34 years), adjusted for stock splits, indicating the magnitude of price appreciation.

indicators.name: Return on Invested Capital
indicators.definition: Rate of earnings on invested capital; measures how effectively a company generates profits from its capital base.

indicators.name: Price Decline from Peak
indicators.definition: The percentage decline in stock price from its highest point, used to assess vulnerability during bear markets (e.g., >50% decline in 1969-70).

indicators.name: Intangible/Earning-Power Valuation
indicators.definition: The portion of a company's market value attributable to intangible assets or earning power above tangible book value, indicating potential overvaluation risk.

indicators.name: Book Value（账面价值）
indicators.definition: 公司净资产的每股价值，以接近账面价值的价格买入可提供安全边际

indicators.name: Price-to-Average-Earnings (3-Year)
indicators.definition: Current stock price divided by the average earnings per share over the past three years; should not exceed 15x

indicators.name: Net Profit Margin
indicators.definition: Net income divided by total revenues, expressed as a percentage, indicating profitability efficiency

indicators.name: Market Cap/Revenues
indicators.definition: Total market capitalization divided by total revenues, a valuation ratio indicating how much investors pay per dollar of revenue

indicators.name: Net Income/Book Value (ROE)
indicators.definition: Net income divided by book value, measuring the return on shareholders' equity

indicators.name: Earnings Multiplier (P/E)
indicators.definition: Price-to-earnings ratio; low-multiplier stocks are considered value investments while high-multiplier stocks are considered glamour investments

indicators.name: Return on Capital
indicators.definition: 资本回报率，衡量公司利用资本创造利润的效率

indicators.name: Dividend Per Share
indicators.definition: 每股股息，衡量公司对股东的现金回报

indicators.name: Organic Revenue Growth
indicators.definition: 有机收入增长率，剔除并购等非内生因素后的真实收入增长

indicators.name: Consecutive Earnings Growth Years
indicators.definition: 连续盈利增长年数，衡量公司盈利的持续性和稳定性

indicators.name: Consecutive Dividend Growth Years
indicators.definition: 连续股息增长年数，衡量公司股东回报的持续性

indicators.name: Net Income/Loss
indicators.definition: The company's total profit or loss after all expenses, indicating whether the company is actually profitable

indicators.name: Stock Price Momentum
indicators.definition: The rate at which a stock's price has risen, which can indicate speculative excess when disconnected from fundamentals

indicators.name: Debt Sustainability
